decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the thermal gradient under the chip. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution, lowering the localized peak temperature under the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conduction resistance becomes negligible compared to boundary convection/resistance (though boundaries are Dirichlet here)
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study
